commit.message:
Fix performance issues in news and filings backfill

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in backfill_news_polygon,
  backfill_news_finnhub, and backfill_8k_for_ticker, replacing per-record
  execute() loops. News is the highest-volume dataset so this has the
  most impact.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_8k_filings to reduce pagination round-trips,
  mirroring the same fix applied to corporate actions endpoints.

- Eliminate redundant dict copy in _extract_sentiment: was doing
  enriched = dict(article) on every article before adding 2 keys.
  Since news.py re-extracts sentiment from insights itself, the copy was
  wasted work. Now mutates in-place instead.

commit.message:
Fix memory leak in corporate actions backfill

- Cache tenacity retry wrapper as self._retrying_execute in PolygonClient.__init__
  instead of recreating a new closure on every HTTP request. Hundreds of wrapper
  objects were accumulating during pagination loops, causing GC pressure and slowness.

- Add limit=1000 param to fetch_dividends, fetch_splits, fetch_short_interest to
  reduce pagination round-trips (Polygon default page size is small).

- Use executemany() for batch inserts in all three backfill_*_for_ticker functions
  instead of a per-record execute() loop.

- Wrap PolygonClient in a with context manager in run_full_backfill so the
  httpx.Client connection pool is always released on exit.

- Add regression tests: retry wrapper identity check across requests, limit param
  assertions on all three corporate actions fetch methods.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/issues/38779)

Currently the ttnn::pad op does not support ND sharding.

hangs in the sharded-optimized path and the erroneous use of

commit.message:
Develop (#62)

* feat: enhance verifier, log traceability, and data source mapping

Verifier Enhancement: Implemented dynamic column discovery for exports, allowing users to select any column from the loaded dataset.
Log Traceability: Added source field to logs to track data origin (e.g., specific HF dataset or manual upload).
Session Management: Improved sessionUid consistency with auto-session creation in Firebase and discovery of "hidden" session IDs directly from logs in the Verifier.
Input Mapping: Fixed query field to preserve original input from selected columns instead of using AI-inferred values in both regular and deep modes.
MCQ Support: Added specialized MCQ column selection and formatting for both HuggingFace and manual data, converting dictionary or list options into readable text.
Multi-turn Refinement: Added configurable limits for multi-turn trace processing and filtered out empty system messages from datasets.

* Initial plan

* feat: preserve original dataset answers in deep reasoning pipeline
- Added [getRowExpectedOutput](cci:1://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/App.tsx:439:4-493:6) helper to [App.tsx](cci:7://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/App.tsx:0:0-0:0) to intelligently extract ground truth answers from HuggingFace, manual uploads, and chat formats.
- Updated [deepReasoningService.ts](cci:7://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/services/deepReasoningService.ts:0:0-0:0) to accept an `expectedAnswer` and use it as the final response when the rewriter phase is disabled.
- Restored and added explicit typing to the [getRowContent](cci:1://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/App.tsx:319:4-437:6) helper in [App.tsx](cci:7://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/App.tsx:0:0-0:0) to fix previous logic breakage and lint errors.
- Integrated the answer preservation logic into the core generation flow, ensuring reasoning traces align with existing ground truth.

* feat: augment reasoning context with gold answers while preserving clean training data
- Updated [deepReasoningService.ts](cci:7://file:///home/mkurman/gitlab/ai/synthlabs-reasoning-generator/services/deepReasoningService.ts:0:0-0:0) to support `originalQuery` and `expectedAnswer` parameters.
- Augmented agent input with `[EXPECTED ANSWER]` in generator mode to provide better context for reasoning traces.
- Ensured training data fields (`query`, `full_seed`) use the clean `originalQuery` to prevent gold answer leakage.
- Added comprehensive trimming to final answers, queries, and assistant message formatting.
- Fixed `App.tsx` generation logic to pass both augmented input for agents and clean input for logs.

* Initial plan

* feat(verifier): implement inline editing, AI rewriting, and settings sync

Inline Editing: Added the ability to edit query, reasoning, and answer fields directly within the
VerifierPanel
 with save/undo functionality.
AI Rewriting: Integrated a new VerifierRewriterService to allow one-click AI improvements for individual fields.
Settings Sync: Synchronized the rewriter's provider and model settings with the main system engine, adding support for dynamic providers (Featherless, Chutes, etc.) and granular retry/delay controls.
Robust Parsing: Implemented a
cleanResponse
 helper to support JSON output formats ({"response": "..."}) as requested in the updated rewriter prompts.
Protocol Core: Updated rewriter prompts to strictly enforce the Stenographic Reasoning Protocol using the system's symbolic lexicon.

* feat(prompts): add file-based prompt management and set selection

BREAKING CHANGE: All hardcoded prompts moved from constants.ts to /prompts directory.
- Adds PromptService for dynamic template loading
- Adds Symbolic prompt set (Aetheric Weaver persona)
- Adds prompt selection tab to Settings
- Fixes reload persistence of selected prompt sets

* Initial plan

* fix(prompts): update to non-deprecated Vite glob API and simplify type casting

Co-authored-by: mkurman <76703881+mkurman@users.noreply.github.com>

- Implement dedicated Reasoning Column selector for Converter mode to support pre-existing logic
- Fix visible <input_query> tags in UI by adding a clean 'display' format to extraction logic
- Ensure ground truth answers are preserved in Regular Converter mode, matching Deep Mode behavior
- Reinforce JSON instructions in Regular Mode prompts to ensure robust parsing across all prompt sets
- Refactor data extraction logic in App.tsx to be shared across regular and deep engines

* feat: add custom prompt sets (synth-compact, synth-expanded, synth-prose) (#23)

Adds 3 new prompt sets following the directory structure from the README:

- synth-compact: Dense, minimal prose for factual/lookup tasks
- synth-expanded: Flowing prose for creative/conversational output
- synth-prose: Structured markdown sections for technical/analytical work

Each set includes complete generator/, converter/, verifier/ subdirectories
with meta.json metadata supporting the "extends" inheritance pattern.

Also adds meta.json to default/ and symbolic/ sets for consistency.

* feat: add task classification and auto-routing infrastructure

Adds infrastructure for automatically routing queries to appropriate prompt
sets based on task type classification.

## New Features

### TaskClassifierService
- Heuristic mode: Free, keyword-based pattern matching with word boundaries
- LLM mode: More accurate classification using configured model
- Configurable confidence threshold
- Custom task→prompt mappings (math→synth-prose, creative→synth-expanded, etc.)

### PromptService Enhancements
- Inheritance chain support via "extends" in meta.json
- hasMetaFile() helper for distinguishing official sets
- Circular inheritance detection with warnings
- Missing parent set warnings

### SettingsService Enhancements
- Auto-routing settings (enabled, method, threshold, LLM model)
- Custom task→prompt mapping storage

### UI Enhancements (SettingsPanel)
- Auto-routing toggle with method selection
- Confidence threshold slider
- Task→prompt mapping editor with reset buttons
- Accessibility improvements (ARIA attributes)

### Integration
- App.tsx: RuntimePromptConfig pattern for React state consistency
- VerifierPanel: promptSet propagation for rewriting
- DeepReasoningService: promptSet parameter throughout pipeline

task/#1055 - Fix centering on login and forgot password pages using mx-auto

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(header): explicit logo width 42px + logo-to-title gap tests

- layout.css: change .cncf-logo-wrapper img width: auto → width: 42px
  (explicit sizing eliminates subpixel rendering inconsistencies that
  caused the title to appear at different distances from the logo)
- layout.css: remove redundant white-space: nowrap from .site-title a
  (inherited from .site-title h1 — was redundant)
- layout.css: remove object-fit: contain (not needed for square 42×42 img)
- header.spec.ts: add logo-to-title gap test (4–14px = ~0.5rem)
- cross-site-header.spec.ts: add 42×42 logo size test for img and svg
- cross-site-header.spec.ts: add logo-to-title gap consistency test
  across all 3 sites (gap must be within ±2px of each other)

Root cause: width: auto on a square SVG-as-img can render at a
slightly different width than expected depending on browser subpixel
rounding, causing the gap between logo and title to differ from
people-website (which uses explicit width: 42px on SVG).

These tests would have caught the spacing regression before merge
if they had been written AND run. Tests written but never executed
provide zero protection.

commit.message:
[Pytorch] Add ignore_raw_node option to GraphPickler (#176939)

Summary:
The Graph Pickler raises "Unexpected raw Node during pickling" when it encounters a raw `torch.fx.Node` during serialization. This occurs in some backward graphs (e.g. IDXCVR model__27_backward_7) where metadata contains Node references that haven't been converted to `_NodePickleData`.

This diff adds an `ignore_raw_node` option to `GraphPickler.Options` that, when enabled, silently pickles unexpected raw Nodes as `None` instead of raising an `AssertionError`. This allows graph serialization to finish even when graphs contain these edge-case references.

Test Plan: buck test fbcode//mtia/accuracy/training/tests:test_graph_serialize; arc lint — no new issues

Reviewed By: aorenste

Differential Revision: D95510476

Pull Request resolved: https://github.com/pytorch/pytorch/pull/176939

commit.message:
Migrate transformer operations to plain functions with bind_function syntax (#37812)

### Ticket
Following pattern from #37584

### Problem description
Transformer operations used deprecated struct-based `register_operation`
pattern. Required migration to plain functions with
`bind_function<"name">` template syntax.

### What's changed

Migrated 15 transformer operations across 6 operation types from
struct-based to plain functions:

**Operations converted:**
- attention_softmax (2 variants: regular + in-place)
- concatenate_heads
- split_query_key_value_and_split_heads  
- sdpa_windowed
- sdpa (7 operations: scaled_dot_product_attention, chunked variants,
joint, ring_joint, flash_mla variants, ring_distributed)
- sdpa_decode (4 operations: decode, paged variants, mla variants)

**Pattern applied:**

```cpp
// Before: struct-based with register_operation
struct ExecuteAttentionSoftmax {
    static ttnn::Tensor invoke(...);
};
constexpr auto attention_softmax = ttnn::register_operation<
    "ttnn::transformer::attention_softmax",
    ExecuteAttentionSoftmax>();

// After: plain function
namespace ttnn::transformer {
    ttnn::Tensor attention_softmax(...);
}

// Nanobind uses direct function pointer (simplified for single-signature functions)
ttnn::bind_function<"attention_softmax">(
    mod, doc,
    &ttnn::transformer::attention_softmax,
    args...);
```

**Changes:**
1. **Nanobind files**: Updated from `bind_registered_operation` to
`bind_function<"name">` template syntax. All single-signature functions
use direct function pointers without `overload_t`/`overload_cast`
wrappers for cleaner, simpler code.
2. **Operation headers**: Replaced `ExecuteXXX` structs with plain
function declarations in `ttnn::transformer` namespace
3. **Operation implementations**: Converted `ExecuteXXX::invoke()`
methods to plain functions (logic unchanged), removed empty namespace
declarations
4. **Removed**: All `register_operation<>` registrations

**Files modified (12):**
- attention_softmax (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)
- concatenate_heads (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)
- split_query_key_value_and_split_heads (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)
- sdpa_windowed (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)
- sdpa (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)
- sdpa_decode (.hpp, .cpp, nanobind.cpp)

**Code improvements:**
- Eliminated ~300 lines of boilerplate code
- Simplified nanobind bindings for all single-signature operations
- Consistent pattern across all transformer operations
- Zero API changes

Follows softmax/layernorm pattern with simplified bindings.

tests](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml/badge.svg?branch=copilot/migrate-transformer-operations)](https://github.com/tenstorrent/tt-metal/actions/workflows/sanity-tests.yaml?query=branch:copilot/migrate-transformer-operations)

commit.message:
Initial foundry-networking repository structure

- scenarios/scenarios.md: 12 networking test scenarios with Mermaid diagrams
  covering inbound (PE, IP filtering), outbound (managed VNet, BYO VNet,
  managed PE, hub-spoke), hybrid (VPN Gateway, App Gateway), and infra
  (DNS validation, trusted services)
- infra/main.bicep: orchestrator deploying VNet, Foundry, Storage, Cosmos DB,
  AI Search, Key Vault, Private Endpoints, DNS zones, Bastion + test VM
- infra/modules/: 9 reusable Bicep modules (vnet, foundry, storage, cosmosdb,
  ai-search, keyvault, private-endpoint, private-dns-zone, bastion,
  log-analytics)
- infra_deploy.ps1: main deployment script with provider registration, RG
  creation, Bicep deployment, RBAC, and VM access configuration
- lab_start.ps1 / lab_stop.ps1: start/stop lab resources to manage costs
- foundry_info.ps1: display lab resource status and networking config
- get_logs.ps1: retrieve diagnostic logs from Log Analytics
